response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior full-stack software developer with a strong web, backend, and developer-tooling background, but the role requires deep hardware-adjacent expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in the resume. Brief low-latency systems work in DLang and some C/C++ experience at National Instruments are the closest touchpoints, but they fall far short of principal-level performance architecture for deep-learning workloads.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career (15+ years) meeting the years-of-experience bar with a BS in CS
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, fibers) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and technical teaching/workshops
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization, GPU, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator workload background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, misaligned with a principal hardware performance architect track
